target: 3. Bonds of Best Friends
Sorry to keep you waiting.
Ah, Hizuru-chan! Did you go to the bathroom or something?
I just dropped my luggage off at the inn and came right out, though.
Really? I feel like I waited a really long time, though.
...I suppose perceived time differs depending on age.
Huh? What?
No, it's nothing. So, where on the island are you going to guide me?
Hmm, where should we go?
Don't tell me... after insisting that much, you didn't have any plan at all...?
Then, wanna go see the Hero of Justice?
Huh?
(Does she mean a fictional hero? No... something like that isn't likely to be stimulating.)
(Well, anything is fine right now. As long as Hoine is satisfied and lets me go quickly.)
Ah, that's fine. Let me meet this so-called Hero of Justice.

Got it!
Come on, follow me!
She's pointlessly energetic...
(Ryunosuke said that this island has changed a bit too, but...)
(It's nothing of the sort. It's almost exactly the same as when I lived here.)
(A hometown that always welcomes you with an unchanging appearance--)
(Well, it's a bit of a cliché expression, but it's not a bad thing. Having a place to return to, that is.)
Hmm hm hm~♪
(...Even if this kind of existence, which is like unreasonableness taken form, also attacks you.)
Hey, hey, Hizuru-chan!
What?
Coming up with stories is your job, right, Hizuru-chan? What kind of stories do you write?
Even if you ask "what kind". Your question is too broad for me to answer.
Papa showed me before, but it was full of difficult kanji so I couldn't read it. The letters were small too.
Instead of stuff like that, couldn't you write something like a picture book? If you did that, I could read it too!
(A picture book... To break out of a slump, maybe jumping into a completely unknown genre wouldn't be a bad idea...)
(No, a picture book is out of the question. My thoughts are already being poisoned by Hoine.)
Ah, it's Mio-san and Tokiko-san! Heeey!
Hello, Hoine-chan.
Hello. You're always so energetic, huh.
And who might this be...?

This is Hizuru-chan!
She's Dad's older sister!
I am Ryunosuke Minakata's older sister... Hizuru Minakata.
Ah, Ryunosuke-san's older sister! I'm Mio Kofune.
I'm Tokiko Hishigata.
(Kofune and Hishigata...)
Ah, from Western Restaurant Kofune and Hishigata Clinic. When I lived on the island, I was often indebted to both of your families.
(Their impressions have changed surprisingly from before. I suppose at this age, it's not uncommon to become like a completely different person after a few years.)
Mio-san and Tokiko-san are super close. They do everything together.
Doing everything together is a bit... I don't think we're together *that* much.
Hehe, but we met yesterday too. And we promised to study together tomorrow, right?
T-That's because... we need to start making progress on our summer vacation assignments soon... And if I'm by myself... y'know?
Yes, yes. I'll properly teach you the parts you don't understand.
Thanks~! I'll give you something as thanks next time.
It's fine. Being with you is fun, Mio.
...You are able to unsparingly offer your own time for the sake of others. Without compensation, of course.
Huh?
Furthermore, this isn't a one-sided service either. Am I wrong?
O-Of course it isn't! If Tokiko helps me, then I'll help Tokiko just as much!
Mio…
Hmm... So-called best friends, huh.
B-Best friends...? That's a bit embarrassing. But it's not wrong, right?
Yeah, it's not wrong.
(Putting it into words might make it sound cheap, but...)
(But if you dig deeper, it's one of those profound themes that you couldn't fully write about even if you spent your whole life on it.)
(However, even between best friends, it's not uncommon for that relationship to unravel due to some trigger.)
(A story about being betrayed by a best friend, huh. It's not an unusable theme.)
(But for what reason would they betray them?)
(A romantic entanglement? No, what I want to write isn't a melodrama.)
Um… Hizuru-san?
I'll ask point-blank. If one of you betrayed the other, what would you do?
Huh!?
Betrayal…?
Yes, I'd like to hear what you two think.
E-Even if you suddenly ask us that... There's no way Tokiko would do something like that, and of course I wouldn't either...!
Use your imagination! What do you think you have the ability to think for!
Eeh...
...At first, I'd be angry. But I'd also be sad... I'd worry a lot about why they did it...
In the end, I think I'd end up forgiving them.
Yeah... I'm the same as Tokiko.
Even if it was the most cruel betrayal imaginable?
Let's see... For Tokiko to betray me, there would have to be a really good reason.
I'd listen to what she has to say, and we'd solve it together. I don't want to part ways with Tokiko over a fight.
Besides, if... I were the one doing the betraying...
Unless it was something extremely serious, I think we would talk it over together before I betrayed her.
Yeah, that's what I thought!
(They'd get angry but forgive them... They'd consult them before betraying them...)
(How ordinary!! There's no entertainment value in that at all!!)
(There are circumstances in this world that you can't easily consult others about. No matter how strong your bond is, there are things you cannot resist.)
(But... the smiles they direct at each other seem to be saying, "No such thing exists"...)
(Is this what it means to be "best friends"? With a strong bond, you can overcome anything.)
(Come to think of it, this is a concept I haven't paid much attention to until now. In that sense, it has a certain freshness to it...)
Thank you. That was helpful.
Helpful...? Helpful for what, exactly...
Ah, don't worry about it.
O-Okay...
(Young... What truly young and straightforward answers.)
(There are worries in this world that you simply can't tell anyone. No matter how strong your bond is, there are things you cannot resist.)
(But, if there is a solid relationship of trust that can overcome even that...)
(I see... That's not a bad theme.)
(The problem is that I am completely unfamiliar with the relationship of "best friends".)
I wonder what's wrong? She's been staring up at the sky deep in thought for a while now...
Maybe she's just a little eccentric...
